summary: Matched a set of previously stubbed Crazy Hand animation callback functions in ftCh_Init.c. Each implementation follows the same small duplicate pattern: call ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj), and when it returns 0, transition to the appropriate Crazy Hand init/unknown state function. The header was updated with prototypes for the transition helpers needed by these newly matched callbacks.

key_files.role: Replaced many Crazy Hand /// #function stubs with matched animation callback implementations.
Added prototypes for transition/init functions called by the newly implemented animation callbacks.

what_changed: Implemented ftCh_Entry_Anim, ftCh_Damage_Anim, ftCh_WaitSweep_Anim, ftCh_SweepLoop_Anim, ftCh_SweepWait_Anim, ftCh_Walk2_Anim, ftCh_WalkShoot_Anim, ftCh_Drill_Anim, ftCh_RockCrushDown_Anim, ftCh_PaperCrush_Anim, ftCh_Poke2_Anim, ftCh_FingerBeamStart_Anim, ftCh_BackPunch_Anim, ftCh_Grab_Anim, ftCh_Squeezing0_Anim, and ftCh_TagCrush_Anim.
Each newly matched animation function checks whether animation frames remain with ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) == 0.
Most callbacks transition to ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015BC88(gobj) when the animation finishes.

Some callbacks transition to more specific init functions: ftCh_Damage_Anim calls ftCh_Init_80157170, ftCh_SweepWait_Anim calls ftCh_Init_8015764C, ftCh_PaperCrush_Anim calls ftCh_Init_801583E4, and ftCh_Grab_Anim calls ftCh_Init_8015A030.
Added header declarations for ftCh_Init_80157170, ftCh_Init_8015764C, ftCh_Init_801583E4, and ftCh_Init_8015A030.
smart_moves: Recognized that many Crazy Hand animation callbacks were duplicate one-branch functions, allowing a broad set of stubs to be matched with a consistent minimal pattern.
Kept the functions simple and callback-shaped: no local variables, no extra control flow, and direct transition calls only when ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining returns 0.
Updated ftCh_Init.h only for the helper functions that newly became referenced, preserving address-annotated prototype ordering.
decomp_lessons: For fighter animation callbacks, a common matching pattern is: if ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) == 0, call the next state/init function.
Duplicate boss/fighter callbacks can often be matched by comparing neighboring state-machine entries and reusing the same frame-finished transition shape.
When newly matched functions reference previously undeclared local init helpers, add address-commented prototypes to the relevant character init header rather than relying on implicit declarations.
State callback names in this area follow the ftCh_<State>_<Phase> convention, where phase includes Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll.
naming_conventions: Crazy Hand symbols use the ftCh_ prefix.
Animation callback functions use the suffix _Anim, for example ftCh_WalkShoot_Anim and ftCh_TagCrush_Anim.
State helper/init functions with unknown semantic names retain address-based names such as ftCh_Init_80157170 and ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015BC88.
Header prototypes include address comments such as /* 157170 */ before the function declaration.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use the exact comparison ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) == 0 for completed-animation branches.
Avoid unnecessary temporaries in these tiny callbacks; a direct if statement and direct transition call is the matching shape shown in the diff.
For duplicate animation-end callbacks, vary only the target transition function while preserving the same control-flow skeleton.
Group matching work around repeated state-machine callback slots to turn many /// # stubs into small matched C functions quickly.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR merged quickly with no visible reviewer-requested changes in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find other ftCrazyHand /// #*_Anim stubs that may use the same ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining completion pattern.
Search other boss/fighter init files for duplicate animation callbacks that transition to a shared idle or grab helper.
Compare ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015BC88 callers to infer its semantic role and possibly rename it once evidence is stronger.
